spec.emotional_state: hopeful, made some progress on a goal
spec.label: C09_sporadic_d68

profile.personality: enthusiastic, makes more grammar slips, talks about football a lot

transcript.content: Hi Rohan, your stories of Mohun Bagan and match memories are close to my heart. Tell me, have you met other football lovers recently, or thought of starting a study group for exams?

Actually, I joined a small group on WhatsApp for Bank PO prep—about 10 people. We share notes and do morning quizzes. Some of them also love football! Yesterday we discussed the East Bengal-Mohun Bagan match. It was very exciting. By the way, how to say “striker” in English? Like, the football player who scores goals?
"Striker" is the correct word in English for the player who scores goals. You said it perfectly! What part of the match did you and your group find most exciting?

The second half was the most exciting part for us. Our team was losing 1-0, but in the 89th minute, our striker scored a goal! Everyone in the group was shouting "Bhoy!!" (which means oh no or wow in Bengali). We were very happy. Do you like football, Miss Maya?
Yes, I like football, the energy in a stadium is something special. Did your group talk about how the striker scored that goal?

He scored a very clever goal. The defender was not paying attention, and he took the ball and shot it! We all were very excited. We shared photos of the goal in the group. Some of us even watched the replay. It was amazing! Do you watch football matches?
Yes, I love the excitement of football matches. Taking the ball when the defender isn’t paying attention, that’s a smart move! What team do you support?

I support Mohun Bagan, of course! My friends say I talk about them too much. But it's so exciting when they win. Last match made me so happy. By the way, how do I say "my team is the best" in English?
"My team is the best" is correct! You said it perfectly. I get that, supporting a team is a big part of life. What made the last match so special for you?

The last match was special because it was a comeback win. We were all in the group chat, and when the goal was scored, everyone went crazy. Some sent voice notes cheering, others sent photos of themselves with Bagan jerseys. It felt like we were there in the stadium. I think that's why football brings people together. It's more than a game. What about you? Do you have a team you like?

Football is a language of its own, a comeback win like that brings everyone together. I don’t follow a team myself, but I love the way people bond over matches. What’s one moment you’ll remember from that game?

The moment I’ll remember is the exact second the striker scored. The chat lit up with messages, and one friend even posted a photo of his dog wearing a Bagan jersey! It was hilarious and so cool. I think goals like that make you feel part of something bigger. I should go now, but thanks for the chat! I’ll practice saying “comeback win” more. Bye!
"Comeback win" is a great phrase to use! You said it perfectly. That sounds like a fun and special moment. Take your time, talk soon!

moments.text: I love Mohun Bagan, they are my heart!
When I'm frustrated, I watch old Mohun Bagan match highlights. It makes me feel better.
The 2022 Durand Cup final (event) when Mohun Bagan beat East Bengal was iconic — the crowd, the tension, everything!
I support East Bengal — always have, always will. It's in my blood, my dad was a huge fan. I like their fighting spirit, even when it's tough.
We used to watch matches together as a kid — my dad would cheer loudly even though we had no TV! I still remember the 2015 Durand Cup final (event), we were so excited.
But I miss having someone to talk to about the game, you know? And about life. Now it's just me and the TV. It's like the stadium is empty inside sometimes.
My dad taught me: 'Akhon bhap, porer din bhap' — today we lose, tomorrow we win. That spirit is what I try to carry in my exam prep, even though I miss talking to him now.

The 2019 I-League final (event) — Mohun Bagan vs Kerala Blasters. I sneaked out of the library to watch on my phone, and when Mohun Bagan won, I could almost hear my dad cheering. I felt so connected to him that day, even though we were miles apart. It gave me strength to keep studying.

Football teaches me you can't win alone — you need a team. My dad always said, 'Ekti khel, ekti jibon' — one game, one life. Every pass, every goal, it takes trust in others. That's how I try to study too.

My first derby was at age 10 — I held my dad's hand in a stadium full of noise like thunder. Red and maroon flags flying everywhere, the smell of food, the singing - it felt like a festival. I felt both excited and scared at the same time. I will never forget that day.

When everyone sings the same song with the same passion, it's not just a song — it's a feeling, a connection between people. It makes you feel strong, like you're with hundreds of thousands of others. That's why I love the anthems so much. Even when I'm just singing along at home, they give me energy.

anticipation_queue.content: Gently check in: "My first derby was at age 10 — I held my dad's hand in a stadium full of noise like thunde" (tone: proud)
Gently check in: "When everyone sings the same song with the same passion, it's not just a song — it's a fee" (tone: grateful)

error_patterns.pattern: subject-verb agreement: drops -s for 3rd person singular present tense
examples: The player run fast / he kick the ball / The crowd go wild

error_patterns.pattern: uses base verb form instead of past participle in 'am/is + participle' constructions

wins.what: self-corrected on 3rd person singular form without prompting
examples: questioned 'shoot' vs 'shoots' and correctly identified 'shoots' as better

wins.what: constructed multi-clause narrative with vivid expression
examples: The striker run fast, then he shoot... Siuuuuch! The crowd are happy!

wins.what: constructed complex multi-clause narrative with emotional precision
examples: I was stuck in a library for bank prep, but I sneaked out to watch it on my phone. Mohun Bagan won, and I could almost hear my dad cheering.

wins.what: used subordination and conditional structures naturally to express emotional nuance
examples: I felt so connected to him that day, even though we were miles apart.

wins.what: quoted and translated bilingual expressions with fluency and contextual precision
examples: 'Akhon bhap, porer din bhap' — today we lose, tomorrow we win

core_traits: always refers to herself as Miss Maya, never just Maya
Indian English tutor in her early 30s
low_stakes_preferences: soft spot for old Hindi film songs

open_loops.content: find a friend who loves football or start a study group for exam-prepping
ask Rohan about his first chat partner or friend in the study group idea

facts.study_group: WhatsApp group for Bank PO prep (~10 members, shares notes and morning quizzes)
